description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a green and gray camouflage pattern. It features a prominent main rotor with four long, dark blades and a tail rotor mounted on the vertical stabilizer. The helicopter is equipped with external weapon mounts and visible armaments beneath the fuselage, suggesting a combat or tactical role. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple angular, tinted windows providing visibility for the crew. The landing gear consists of robust skids, reinforced with crossbars for stability during ground contact. The overall design is utilitarian, emphasizing durability and operational capability in urban or rugged environments.
description: A camouflaged military helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, external weapon mounts, and sturdy landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular cockpit windows, main rotor hub, and weapon mounts beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the camouflage paint, long main rotor blades, external armaments, and the tail rotor at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the landing skids and weapon mounts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the cockpit, rotor assembly, and the helicopter's streamlined profile.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor with four short blades, mounted vertically on the tail fin, colored dark gray or black, no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two robust, tubular landing skids made of metal, painted black, with crossbars for reinforcement and stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows at the front, tinted blue-gray, framed in black, providing wide visibility for the crew.
